UAE's non-oil foreign trade up 13% in first half of year to Dh1.9 trillion

Mohammed bin Rashid: Today, we reviewed the UAE’s non-oil foreign trade results for the first half of 2026, and the figures are truly historic. For the first time, our non-oil foreign trade has come close to the AED 2 trillion mark in six months, reaching AED 1.937 trillion—an increase of 13.1% compared to the same period last year. Meanwhile, our non-oil exports rose to a new all-time high of AED 452.8 billion. These are more than just trade figures. They reflect the strength and resilience of our economy, the success of our development strategy, and the confidence placed in the UAE by the world.

Dubai's Roads and Transport Authority approves a five-year plan to expand the emirate's soft mobility network through 2030. The plan aims to strengthen links between residential areas and public transport stations. The plan encompasses infrastructure development across 25 residential areas and upgrades to the infrastructure surrounding 63 public transport stations by 2030. Soft mobility elements are currently being implemented in five areas: Dubai Marina, Al Murar, Naif, Al Rigga and Al Muraqqabat.

RT by @moiuae: RT by @moiuae: The Government of Dubai Media Office (GDMO) confirms that the Reuters report regarding explosions in Downtown Dubai is false.
